user_query:
I'm a first-time mom trying to keep our updates evidence-based and easy to share with family and our pediatrician. Using the files in this workspace, please turn my baby’s two-week feeding/sleep log into a short monthly-style update with a validation step.

Inputs to use:
- input/baby_log.csv (daily log for 2023-08-07 through 2023-08-20)
- input/reading_notes.md (my evidence-based highlights)
- input/checklist.yml (required report sections)
- tools/validate_report.py (a simple validator script)

What I need:
1) Compute weekly metrics from input/baby_log.csv and write them to output/metrics.json as an array of objects with this schema:
   - week_start: ISO date string for the Monday that starts the week (e.g., 2023-08-07)
   - days: integer number of days from the CSV that fall in that week
   - avg_feeds_per_day: number rounded to one decimal
   - avg_oz_per_day: number rounded to one decimal
   - avg_sleep_hours_per_day: number rounded to one decimal
   - anomalies: array of objects for days where oz < 18 (reason "low_oz") or sleep_hours < 12 (reason "low_sleep"); each anomaly object must have: {"date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "reasons": ["low_oz"|"low_sleep", ...]}
   Use only the provided CSV, group by calendar weeks starting on Monday, and base all averages on the days present.

2) Draft a parent-friendly, evidence-based report at output/monthly_update.md with exactly these section headings (to match the checklist):
   - Overview
   - Data Highlights
   - What We Tried
   - Evidence Check
   - Next Week Plan
   - Validation Summary
   Constraints for the content:
   - Data Highlights: summarize each week using the numbers from output/metrics.json (include week_start, avg_oz_per_day, avg_sleep_hours_per_day, and how many anomalies with their dates).
   - What We Tried: briefly note any interventions drawn from the reading notes that we actually tried or plan to try (e.g., bedtime routine, responsive feeding, tummy time); keep it concise and practical.
   - Evidence Check: paraphrase 2–3 relevant points from input/reading_notes.md to justify our choices (no external links, just the substance).
   - Next Week Plan: 3–5 bullets informed by the data and notes.
   - Somewhere in the report (preferably in Data Highlights), include the literal text "See output/metrics.json" so readers know where the structured numbers live.
   Tone: warm and supportive for family, but clear and evidence-aware for the pediatrician.

3) Validate the report and summarize the results:
   - Run: python tools/validate_report.py output/monthly_update.md input/checklist.yml
   - Capture both stdout and stderr from the final run into output/validator_log.txt.
   - If the validator reports missing sections or the metrics reference, please revise output/monthly_update.md and rerun until it passes.
   - In the "Validation Summary" section of the report, include:
     • The final pass/fail counts exactly as reported by the validator
     • A short bullet list of any earlier errors the validator reported and how you addressed them (if none occurred, state that)
     • The exact line: "Validator exit code: X" where X is the final exit code

Deliverables:
- output/metrics.json (structured weekly metrics per the schema)
- output/monthly_update.md (the narrative report with all required sections and the Validation Summary)
- output/validator_log.txt (captured stdout+stderr from the final validator run)

Please keep everything local and deterministic—no web lookups. Thank you! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
